how far is the sun from earth

The distance from the Earth to the Sun varies throughout the year as the earth follows its elliptical orbit. However, on average, the distance from the Earth to the Sun is approximately 93 million miles (149.6 million kilometers). This distance is also known as one astronomical unit (AU).

Cats can get rabies, but it is not necessarily common. Rabies is a viral disease that affects the nervous system of mammals, including cats and humans. Rabies is typically contracted through the bite of an infected animal. Cats that have not been vaccinated against rabies can contract the virus if they come into contact with infected wild animals or other infected animals. If you suspect that your cat may have rabies or has been in contact with an animal that has rabies, it is important to seek veterinary care immediately. Symptoms of rabies in cats can include changes in behavior, aggression, drooling, paralysis, and seizures.

Rabies in cats, as well as in other animals and humans, can be very dangerous and even fatal if left untreated. Rabies is a viral disease that affects the central nervous system and can cause inflammation in the brain. It is transmitted through the saliva of an infected animal, typically through a bite.

If a cat is infected with rabies, symptoms can take several weeks to appear but may include fever, seizures, disorientation, aggression, and difficulty walking or swallowing.

Once symptoms appear, there is no known cure for rabies and it is almost always fatal. However, rabies can be prevented through vaccination. It is important to vaccinate cats against rabies, especially if they spend time outdoors or come into contact with other animals. Additionally, if you suspect that your cat has been exposed to rabies, seek veterinary care immediately.

Configuring a Synology NAS (Network Attached Storage) involves several steps. Here are the basic steps on how to configure Synology NAS:

1. Hardware setup: Unpack and set up the NAS device by connecting it to a power source, network, and storage drives.

2. Access the NAS Device: Once the hardware is set up, access the Synology NAS device through a web browser by entering the IP address in the browser’s address bar.

3. Set Up the Network: Configure network settings like setting up IP address, gateway, DNS, etc. It is necessary to set up the network correctly to ensure that other devices can connect to the Synology NAS.

4. User Management: Create user accounts and groups with access privileges. This step helps in enforcing proper access control to your Synology NAS.

5. Shared Folder Creation: Create shared folders on Synology NAS device, which can be accessed by other devices on the same network. Folder permissions can be managed here.

6. Install Synology Packages: Synology provides different packages/apps that you can install based on your requirement, like antivirus, cloud synchronization, multimedia, etc.

7. Backup Configuration: You can also configure the backup settings for Synology NAS, to ensure that important data is backed up regularly.

These are some basic steps involved in configuring Synology NAS. However, the exact steps can vary depending on the model of your Synology NAS device. It is recommended that you refer to the user manual or documentation provided by Synology for detailed information specific to your specific model.
